tool.name: VADER Sentiment Analysis
tool.help:
VADER Sentiment Analysis ========================
Performs sentiment analysis using `VADER <https://github.com/cjhutto/vaderSentiment>`_ (Valence Aware Dictionary and sEntiment Reasoner), a lexicon and rule-based sentiment analysis tool specifically attuned to sentiments expressed in social media and general text.
When to Use VADER -----------------
VADER works well on:
- **Political speeches and debates** — handles rhetorical language, emphasis, and persuasion - **News articles** — captures positive/negative framing - **Social media and opinion text** — originally designed for this domain - **Short informal text** — handles slang, emoticons, punctuation emphasis
VADER may be preferred over model-based approaches (spaCy, Stanza) when:
- You need interpretable, rule-based scores - You don't want to download large language models - You need fast processing of large volumes of text
Scores ------
VADER produces four scores for each text unit:
**Compound** (-1 to +1) Overall sentiment score. Most useful single metric.
- >= +0.05: positive - <= -0.05: negative - between -0.05 and +0.05: neutral
**Positive** (0 to 1) Proportion of text that is positive.
**Negative** (0 to 1) Proportion of text that is negative.
**Neutral** (0 to 1) Proportion of text that is neutral.
Positive + Negative + Neutral = 1.0
Analysis Level --------------
**Per sentence** Splits text into sentences and scores each one individually. Produces a table with one row per sentence.
**Whole document** Scores the entire text as a single unit. Useful for getting an overall sentiment of a document.
Output Formats --------------
**Tabular (TSV)** Tab-separated file with columns: sentence, compound, positive, negative, neutral, label. The label column classifies each sentence as positive/negative/neutral based on the compound score threshold of +/-0.05.
**JSON** Structured JSON with scores and text for each analyzed unit.
